subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, GPI, Freedom House) + Icelandic official (Statistics Iceland, Central Bank, Met Office) + Icelandic press.
A tiny, wealthy, exceptionally stable and safe democracy; the dominant tail is volcanic/seismic (the ongoing Reykjanes eruptions).

Standout features: top-tier institutions, world-leading safety, ~100% renewable energy, against a small/volatile currency, tourism concentration, and a seismic tail. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone on index framing.

economic.notes: Prudent fiscals and sound rebuilt banks, with a small/volatile krona and tourism concentration the caveats.

fiscal_state.notes: The deficit fell sharply with a surplus targeted, under a net-debt ceiling - prudent. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible central bank with full policy control, but a tiny/volatile krona with a capital-control history. (CBI; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The krona has no reserve status. (IMF)

inflation.notes: Sticky/rising in early 2026 (a rate hike to ~7.75%), with the IMF seeing a return toward target. (Statistics Iceland; CBI)

banking_stability.notes: Rebuilt and well-capitalised since 2008, with housing/indexed-mortgage watch-items. (IMF FSAP)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Tourism, fish, and aluminium on cheap geothermal/hydro energy - concentrated and tourism-cyclical, no resource curse. (OECD)

institutional.notes: Top-tier institutions, with small-market media concentration the lone caveat.

statistical_integrity.notes: Statistics Iceland is independent and uncompromised. (WJP)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Professional and low-corruption (a high CPI). (TI CPI 2025)

press_freedom.notes: Free (RSF ~12th), with fishing-firm media-ownership concentration in a small market. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: A very stable, high-trust democracy with strong liberties and relatively favourable demographics.

political_stability.notes: A stable democracy with an orderly 2024 handover. (IPU; RUV)

civil_liberties.notes: Among the world's strongest protections. (Freedom House; WJP)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: ~18% foreign-born and labour-importing, EEA-open, with a 2024 work-permit liberalisation. (OECD)

social_cohesion.notes: Very high trust and egalitarian - a genuine strength. (OECD)

demographics.notes: Relatively healthy fertility (~1.6-1.7) plus immigration-driven growth - favourable versus peers. (Statistics Iceland)

geopolitical.notes: A geographically insulated NATO founder, the world's most peaceful country, with an open account.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO founder (no standing army) with a long US defence pact and Nordic alignment. (NATO)

conflict_involvement.notes: Ranked the world's most peaceful, with no conflict and geographic insulation. (GPI 2025)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account (post-2017 control removal), EEA-aligned. (State Dept ICS)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety, against a volcanic/seismic tail.

climate.notes: A sub-Arctic beneficiary near-term (milder climate, geothermal-rich, very high readiness), but ocean-acidification/fisheries shifts and the AMOC tail erode the gain long-term. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

crime_safety.notes: The world's safest, with violent crime extraordinarily rare. (GPI 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: A state natural-catastrophe fund covers all buildings, stress-tested by the Grindavik eruptions. (NTI)

summary: A tiny, wealthy, exceptionally stable democracy: top-tier institutions, the world's lowest crime, ~100% renewable electricity, prudent public finances, sound rebuilt banks, and high social trust. The caveats are economic concentration (tourism, fish, aluminium) and a small, volatile krona with a capital-control history, and - most distinctively - a volcanic/seismic tail, with the ongoing Reykjanes eruptions displacing the town of Grindavik (covered by a state catastrophe fund). A NATO founder and the world's most peaceful country.
